stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 40 subscribers. The sample mean is 148.3 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 21.4 Mbps. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 148.3 Mbps. 95% CI = [141.5, 155.1]. Point estimate: 148.3 Mbps.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
